# Controlled Exposure Playbook

Organic reach cannot be guaranteed. Exposure can be.

## Controlled exposure sources

### 1. Paid micro-seeding

Use small paid tests to guarantee minimum impressions for specific variants.

Use for:

```text
first-frame tests
hook tests
CTA tests
landing-page/content-market fit
```

Do not use paid seeding to pretend organic virality happened. Use it to collect enough signal.

### 2. Owned audience

Distribute through:

```text
email list
Discord/Slack/community
website/blog
newsletter
personal network
existing profile stories
```

Use for:

```text
qualitative feedback
save/share prompt tests
series positioning
```

### 3. Partner distribution

Use:

```text
collabs
remixes
stitches/duets
expert reactions
creator swaps
community moderators
```

Use for:

```text
borrowed trust
weak-tie spread
new audience sampling
```

### 4. Platform-native cold testing

Use Instagram Trial Reels for risky or new formats. Trial Reels are specifically designed to show content to non-followers first.

Use for:

```text
new hook styles
new topics
non-follower clarity
format experiments
```

### 5. Cross-platform translation

Do not just repost. Translate:

```text
TikTok = search/culture/comment loop
Reels = sends/identity/non-follower tests
Shorts = first-frame + engaged views + search
LinkedIn = professional proof + point of view
```

## Minimum exposure rule

Do not judge a mechanism until it has either:

```text
sufficient organic exposure relative to account median
or controlled exposure through paid/owned/partner/community distribution
```

A zero-reach post is not strong evidence that the creative failed.
